LAW AND MOTION 302, JUN-28-2023. HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JESSICA CHANG'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT AND COUNSEL DISCUSS THE REASON FOR THIS MORNING'S HEARING SPECIFICALLY, RE: BOTH SIDES' APPARENT VIOLATION OF CRC 3.1113(D). AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, COUNSEL IS ADVISED THAT THEY MAY REFILE, WITH PROPERLY SIZED MEMORANDUMS - SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THIS DATE. JUDGE: RICHARD B, ULMER JR., CLERK: R. WOODS, NOT REPORTED. Appearances via Zoom Conference
Kyle Batter, Esq. QUINN EMANUEL URQUHART & SULLIVAN, LLP, (650) 801-5000, kylebatter@quinnemanuel.com for Plaintiffs Siddhanta Dange, Evan Katz, Raylene Chew, and Stephen Shan

Teresa Maciel, CSR# 10134, Steno, 559-514-4905, macielteresa@outlook.com, Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JESSICA CHANG DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Teresa Maciel, CSR# 10134, Steno, 559-514-4905, macielteresa@outlook.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

*AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANT'S AMENDED DEMURRER IS SIGNED.*

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JESSICA CHANG's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's "amended demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. It is not counsels' role to determine whether CCP 430.41 is to be complied with. (See Amd. Trachtman Dec. 4:4-6.) CCP sec. 430.41 requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by August 30, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Hearing RE: Defendant JESSICA CHANG's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Kyle Batter, Esq., QUINN EMANUEL URQUHART & SULLIVAN, LLP, (650) 801-5000, for Plaintiffs.

Laura M. Trachtman, Esq., Trachtman & Trachtman, (212) 918-4750, and Garrett Prybylo, SEYFNIA & PRYBYLO, LLP, (213) 770-4529, for Defendant.

Ruling - Discussed. The court and counsel discuss the reason for this morning's hearing specifically, RE: both sides' apparent violation of CRC 3.1113(d) - length of the Memorandums.

At the conclusion of discussion, counsel is advised that they may refile, with properly sized Memorandums.
